Delphin..personality: A refined, sensitive individual who maintains strict self-control and suppresses emotions rather than expressing them openly. Excitable and easily aroused but rarely manifests anger outwardly, instead turning inward and suffering silently from pent-up feelings. Often intellectually oriented but physically and nervously depleted from sexual excesses or prolonged emotional suppression.
Delphin..mental_state: Marked by dullness, brain-fag, and loss of memory, with a characteristic sensation of numbness or woodenness in the head as if the brain were made of wood. Cannot concentrate, makes mistakes in writing and speaking, and struggles with simple mental tasks like arithmetic after episodes of suppressed indignation. The mind dwells excessively on sexual or grievance-related subjects, leading to further nervous exhaustion.
Delphin..emotional_pattern: The core pattern is suppressed indignation and pent-up wrath that produces profound physical and mental collapse—trembling, sleeplessness, and prostration. Becomes speechless from anger rather than expressing it, then suffers intensely from the consequences of self-control. Deep sensitivity to insult and injustice combined with hypersensitivity at every level—physical, emotional, and sensory—so that the slightest touch or provocation can shatter composure entirely.
